{"directory":"C:\Users\rajku\OneDrive\Documents\ClePro\blocker","payload":{"type":"message.part.updated","properties":{"part":{"id":"prt_ce0a4b7f00017fb6WFgN8XrkSt","sessionID":"ses_31f5b848fffeVoyKgPeamnipLj","messageID":"msg_ce0a4b0590012t4RvZ6G0ooyO5","type":"text","text":"Here is a complete, structured summary of the project:

---

## Project Summary: Focus Blocker

### 1. Project Type

**Browser Extension** — packaged for both **Chrome/Chromium** (Manifest V3) and **Firefox** (Manifest V2 / WebExtensions API). Distributable `.zip` files are present for both targets, including Firefox Android.

---

### 2. Technologies & Frameworks

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Language | Vanilla JavaScript (ES2020+, async/await throughout) |
| Extension APIs | Chrome Extensions API (MV3 `declarativeNetRequest`, `scripting`, `alarms`, `storage`) / Firefox WebExtensions API (MV2 `webRequest`, `webRequestBlocking`) |
| Styling | Plain CSS, Google Fonts (`JetBrains Mono`, `Space Grotesk`) |
| Build tooling | None — no `package.json`, no bundler, no transpiler; raw source files are the distributable |
| Icons/Favicons | DuckDuckGo favicon API (`icons.duckduckgo.com/ip3/`) for blocked site chips |

---

### 3. Top-Level Directory Structure

```
blocker/
├── background.js              # Chrome MV3 service worker (core blocking logic)
├── background-firefox.js      # Firefox MV2 background script (same logic, browser API)
├── characters.js              # Shared module: character definitions & storage helpers
├── manifest.json              # Chrome/Edge manifest (MV3)
├── manifest.firefox.json      # Firefox manifest (MV2)
├── popup/
│   ├── popup.html             # Extension toolbar popup UI
│   ├── popup.js               # Popup controller (status, controls, site list)
│   └── popup.css              # Popup styles
├── blocked/
│   ├── blocked.html           # Full-page block screen (hard block redirect target)
│   ├── blocked.js             # Block page controller (countdown, quotes, character)
│   ├── blocked.css            # Block page styles
│   ├── ippo-sprite.png        # Sprite sheet: Hajime no Ippo character
│   ├── ralph-sprite.png       # Sprite sheet: Ralph (classic)
│   ├── ralph-smash-sprite.png # Sprite sheet: Ralph (ground smash)
│   ├── amongus-sprite.png     # Sprite sheet: Among Us crewmate
│   └── ghost-sprite.png       # Sprite sheet: Ghost
├── icons/
│   ├── icon-16.png
│   ├── icon-48.png
│   └── icon-128.png
├── dist-firefox/              # Built/packaged Firefox extension directory
├── focus-blocker-chrome.zip
├── focus-blocker-firefox.zip
├── focus-blocker-firefox-1.0.3.zip
├── focus-blocker-firefox-1.0.4.zip
└── focus-blocker-firefox-1.0.5.zip
```

---

### 4. Key Configuration Files

**`manifest.json` (Chrome MV3, v1.0.5)**
- Service worker: `background.js`
- Popup: `popup/popup.html`
- Permissions: `storage`, `alarms`, `declarativeNetRequest`, `scripting`, `activeTab`, `<all_urls>`
- Uses dynamic `declarativeNetRequest` rules (no static ruleset)

**`manifest.firefox.json` (Firefox MV2, v1.0.5)**
- Background scripts: `["characters.js", "background.js"]` (loaded together, not a service worker)
- Uses `browser_action` (MV2 naming), `webRequest`/`webRequestBlocking` permissions
- Includes `gecko` + `gecko_android` IDs (`focus-blocker@rajku.dev`), minimum Firefox 109
- Data collection declared as `none`

**No `package.json`** — there is no Node.js toolchain; the project ships raw source directly.

---

### 5. What the Project Does

**Focus Blocker** is a productivity browser extension that blocks distracting websites during two scenarios:

**Automatic Morning Block:** Every day from **6:00 AM to 12:00 PM**, a configurable list of sites (default: `youtube.com`, `x.com`, `reddit.com`) is automatically blocked. `music.youtube.com` is explicitly exempt even when `youtube.com` is blocked.

**Manual Focus Sessions:** The user can trigger timed focus sessions (15m / 30m / 1h / 2h) from the popup. Once started, sessions **cannot be cancelled** — a deliberate friction mechanic to prevent cheating.

**Two blocking modes:**
- **Hard block** — redirects the tab to a full-page `/blocked/blocked.html` screen
- **Soft block** — injects an overlay banner directly into the target page via `scripting.executeScript` (Chrome) or `tabs.executeScript` (Firefox)

**Block screen features:**
- Animated pixel-art sprite characters (5 choices: Ippo, Ralph Classic, Ralph Smash, Among Us Crewmate, Ghost) with per-character color themes applied via CSS custom properties
- Live countdown timer to the end of the block period
- Rotating motivational quotes (Bruce Lee, Tim Ferriss, Aristotle, etc.)

**Popup UI features:**
- Live status indicator (ACTIVE / IDLE) with countdown
- Hard/Soft mode toggle
- Blocked site management: add custom domains, remove non-default sites (default 3 sites are locked)
- Mascot/character selector with sprite previews
- Footer shows morning block schedule","time":{"start":1773295443441,"end":1773295443441}}}}}

Here is a complete, structured summary of the project:

---

## Project Summary: Focus Blocker

### 1. Project Type

**Browser Extension** — packaged for both **Chrome/Chromium** (Manifest V3) and **Firefox** (Manifest V2 / WebExtensions API). Distributable `.zip` files are present for both targets, including Firefox Android.

---

### 2. Technologies & Frameworks

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Language | Vanilla JavaScript (ES2020+, async/await throughout) |
| Extension APIs | Chrome Extensions API (MV3 `declarativeNetRequest`, `scripting`, `alarms`, `storage`) / Firefox WebExtensions API (MV2 `webRequest`, `webRequestBlocking`) |
| Styling | Plain CSS, Google Fonts (`JetBrains Mono`, `Space Grotesk`) |
| Build tooling | None — no `package.json`, no bundler, no transpiler; raw source files are the distributable |
| Icons/Favicons | DuckDuckGo favicon API (`icons.duckduckgo.com/ip3/`) for blocked site chips |

---

### 3. Top-Level Directory Structure

```
blocker/
├── background.js              # Chrome MV3 service worker (core blocking logic)
├── background-firefox.js      # Firefox MV2 background script (same logic, browser API)
├── characters.js              # Shared module: character definitions & storage helpers
├── manifest.json              # Chrome/Edge manifest (MV3)
├── manifest.firefox.json      # Firefox manifest (MV2)
├── popup/
│   ├── popup.html             # Extension toolbar popup UI
│   ├── popup.js               # Popup controller (status, controls, site list)
│   └── popup.css              # Popup styles
├── blocked/
│   ├── blocked.html           # Full-page block screen (hard block redirect target)
│   ├── blocked.js             # Block page controller (countdown, quotes, character)
│   ├── blocked.css            # Block page styles
│   ├── ippo-sprite.png        # Sprite sheet: Hajime no Ippo character
│   ├── ralph-sprite.png       # Sprite sheet: Ralph (classic)
│   ├── ralph-smash-sprite.png # Sprite sheet: Ralph (ground smash)
│   ├── amongus-sprite.png     # Sprite sheet: Among Us crewmate
│   └── ghost-sprite.png       # Sprite sheet: Ghost
├── icons/
│   ├── icon-16.png
│   ├── icon-48.png
│   └── icon-128.png
├── dist-firefox/              # Built/packaged Firefox extension directory
├── focus-blocker-chrome.zip
├── focus-blocker-firefox.zip
├── focus-blocker-firefox-1.0.3.zip
├── focus-blocker-firefox-1.0.4.zip
└── focus-blocker-firefox-1.0.5.zip
```

---

### 4. Key Configuration Files

**`manifest.json` (Chrome MV3, v1.0.5)**
- Service worker: `background.js`
- Popup: `popup/popup.html`
- Permissions: `storage`, `alarms`, `declarativeNetRequest`, `scripting`, `activeTab`, `<all_urls>`
- Uses dynamic `declarativeNetRequest` rules (no static ruleset)

**`manifest.firefox.json` (Firefox MV2, v1.0.5)**
- Background scripts: `["characters.js", "background.js"]` (loaded together, not a service worker)
- Uses `browser_action` (MV2 naming), `webRequest`/`webRequestBlocking` permissions
- Includes `gecko` + `gecko_android` IDs (`focus-blocker@rajku.dev`), minimum Firefox 109
- Data collection declared as `none`

**No `package.json`** — there is no Node.js toolchain; the project ships raw source directly.

---

### 5. What the Project Does

**Focus Blocker** is a productivity browser extension that blocks distracting websites during two scenarios:

**Automatic Morning Block:** Every day from **6:00 AM to 12:00 PM**, a configurable list of sites (default: `youtube.com`, `x.com`, `reddit.com`) is automatically blocked. `music.youtube.com` is explicitly exempt even when `youtube.com` is blocked.

**Manual Focus Sessions:** The user can trigger timed focus sessions (15m / 30m / 1h / 2h) from the popup. Once started, sessions **cannot be cancelled** — a deliberate friction mechanic to prevent cheating.

**Two blocking modes:**
- **Hard block** — redirects the tab to a full-page `/blocked/blocked.html` screen
- **Soft block** — injects an overlay banner directly into the target page via `scripting.executeScript` (Chrome) or `tabs.executeScript` (Firefox)

**Block screen features:**
- Animated pixel-art sprite characters (5 choices: Ippo, Ralph Classic, Ralph Smash, Among Us Crewmate, Ghost) with per-character color themes applied via CSS custom properties
- Live countdown timer to the end of the block period
- Rotating motivational quotes (Bruce Lee, Tim Ferriss, Aristotle, etc.)

**Popup UI features:**
- Live status indicator (ACTIVE / IDLE) with countdown
- Hard/Soft mode toggle
- Blocked site management: add custom domains, remove non-default sites (default 3 sites are locked)
- Mascot/character selector with sprite previews
- Footer shows morning block schedule

---

## Project Summary: Focus Blocker

### 1. Project Type

**Browser Extension** — packaged for both **Chrome/Chromium** (Manifest V3) and **Firefox** (Manifest V2 / WebExtensions API). Distributable `.zip` files are present for both targets, including Firefox Android.

---

### 2. Technologies & Frameworks

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Language | Vanilla JavaScript (ES2020+, async/await throughout) |
| Extension APIs | Chrome Extensions API (MV3 `declarativeNetRequest`, `scripting`, `alarms`, `storage`) / Firefox WebExtensions API (MV2 `webRequest`, `webRequestBlocking`) |
| Styling | Plain CSS, Google Fonts (`JetBrains Mono`, `Space Grotesk`) |
| Build tooling | None — no `package.json`, no bundler, no transpiler; raw source files are the distributable |
| Icons/Favicons | DuckDuckGo favicon API (`icons.duckduckgo.com/ip3/`) for blocked site chips |

---

### 3. Top-Level Directory Structure

```
blocker/
├── background.js              # Chrome MV3 service worker (core blocking logic)
├── background-firefox.js      # Firefox MV2 background script (same logic, browser API)
├── characters.js              # Shared module: character definitions & storage helpers
├── manifest.json              # Chrome/Edge manifest (MV3)
├── manifest.firefox.json      # Firefox manifest (MV2)
├── popup/
│   ├── popup.html             # Extension toolbar popup UI
│   ├── popup.js               # Popup controller (status, controls, site list)
│   └── popup.css              # Popup styles
├── blocked/
│   ├── blocked.html           # Full-page block screen (hard block redirect target)
│   ├── blocked.js             # Block page controller (countdown, quotes, character)
│   ├── blocked.css            # Block page styles
│   ├── ippo-sprite.png        # Sprite sheet: Hajime no Ippo character
│   ├── ralph-sprite.png       # Sprite sheet: Ralph (classic)
│   ├── ralph-smash-sprite.png # Sprite sheet: Ralph (ground smash)
│   ├── amongus-sprite.png     # Sprite sheet: Among Us crewmate
│   └── ghost-sprite.png       # Sprite sheet: Ghost
├── icons/
│   ├── icon-16.png
│   ├── icon-48.png
│   └── icon-128.png
├── dist-firefox/              # Built/packaged Firefox extension directory
├── focus-blocker-chrome.zip
├── focus-blocker-firefox.zip
├── focus-blocker-firefox-1.0.3.zip
├── focus-blocker-firefox-1.0.4.zip
└── focus-blocker-firefox-1.0.5.zip
```

---

### 4. Key Configuration Files

**`manifest.json` (Chrome MV3, v1.0.5)**
- Service worker: `background.js`
- Popup: `popup/popup.html`
- Permissions: `storage`, `alarms`, `declarativeNetRequest`, `scripting`, `activeTab`, `<all_urls>`
- Uses dynamic `declarativeNetRequest` rules (no static ruleset)

**`manifest.firefox.json` (Firefox MV2, v1.0.5)**
- Background scripts: `["characters.js", "background.js"]` (loaded together, not a service worker)
- Uses `browser_action` (MV2 naming), `webRequest`/`webRequestBlocking` permissions
- Includes `gecko` + `gecko_android` IDs (`focus-blocker@rajku.dev`), minimum Firefox 109
- Data collection declared as `none`

**No `package.json`** — there is no Node.js toolchain; the project ships raw source directly.

---

### 5. What the Project Does

**Focus Blocker** is a productivity browser extension that blocks distracting websites during two scenarios:

**Automatic Morning Block:** Every day from **6:00 AM to 12:00 PM**, a configurable list of sites (default: `youtube.com`, `x.com`, `reddit.com`) is automatically blocked. `music.youtube.com` is explicitly exempt even when `youtube.com` is blocked.

**Manual Focus Sessions:** The user can trigger timed focus sessions (15m / 30m / 1h / 2h) from the popup. Once started, sessions **cannot be cancelled** — a deliberate friction mechanic to prevent cheating.

**Two blocking modes:**
- **Hard block** — redirects the tab to a full-page `/blocked/blocked.html` screen
- **Soft block** — injects an overlay banner directly into the target page via `scripting.executeScript` (Chrome) or `tabs.executeScript` (Firefox)

**Block screen features:**
- Animated pixel-art sprite characters (5 choices: Ippo, Ralph Classic, Ralph Smash, Among Us Crewmate, Ghost) with per-character color themes applied via CSS custom properties
- Live countdown timer to the end of the block period
- Rotating motivational quotes (Bruce Lee, Tim Ferriss, Aristotle, etc.)

**Popup UI features:**
- Live status indicator (ACTIVE / IDLE) with countdown
- Hard/Soft mode toggle

part.text:
Here's the summary:

---

## Focus Blocker — Project Summary

### 1. Project Type
**Browser Extension** — targets both Chrome/Chromium (Manifest V3) and Firefox (Manifest V2), including Firefox for Android.

### 2. Technologies Used
- **Language:** Vanilla JavaScript (ES2020+, no framework, no bundler)
- **Extension APIs:** Chrome `declarativeNetRequest` / Firefox `webRequest` + `webRequestBlocking`
- **Styling:** Plain CSS with Google Fonts (`JetBrains Mono`, `Space Grotesk`)
- **Build tooling:** None — raw source files are the distributable

### 3. Top-Level Structure
```
blocker/
├── background.js              # Chrome MV3 service worker
├── background-firefox.js      # Firefox MV2 background script
├── characters.js              # Shared character/storage helpers
├── manifest.json              # Chrome manifest (MV3)
├── manifest.firefox.json      # Firefox manifest (MV2)
├── popup/                     # Toolbar popup UI (html/js/css)
├── blocked/                   # Full-page block screen + sprite assets
├── icons/                     # Extension icons (16/48/128px)
├── dist-firefox/              # Packaged Firefox build directory
└── focus-blocker-*.zip        # Release zips (Chrome + Firefox versions)
```

### 4. Key Files
- **No `package.json`** — no Node.js toolchain at all
- `manifest.json` — Chrome MV3, v1.0.5, id: `focus-blocker@rajku.dev`
- `manifest.firefox.json` — Firefox MV2, includes Gecko + Android IDs
- Entry points: `background.js` (service worker), `popup/popup.js`, `blocked/blocked.js`

### 5. What It Does
**Focus Blocker** is a productivity extension that blocks distracting websites in two ways:

- **Automatic morning block:** 6 AM–12 PM daily, blocks a configurable site list (YouTube, X, Reddit by default; `music.youtube.com` is exempt)
- **Manual focus sessions:** User-triggered timed sessions (15m/30m/1h/2h) that **cannot be cancelled** once started — intentional friction

**Two block modes:**
- **Hard block** — redirects to a full-page block screen with animated pixel-art sprites (Ippo, Ralph, Among Us crewmate, Ghost), a countdown timer, and motivational quotes
- **Soft block** — injects an overlay banner into the target page

summary.title: Project structure summary request for ClePro\blocker
